generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T ARUM DRACUNCULUS. A. dracunculus, Willd. Nat order , Araceæ. Preparation , Tincture from the root. Authority. 1 , Journ. d. l. Soc. Gal., IV, 114: effects of cutting and pounding the root; Dr. Demeures; 2 , Bibliothèque Homopathique, III, 192, effects of tearing up the leaves and stems; Dr. Pitet. ► Styptic and metallic taste, mingled with bitterness at the back of the mouth, [1] . ► Prickings as with thorns in the fingers of the right hand, [1] . ► Feeling as if the hand were plunged among nettles, [1] . ► On rubbing the fingers together, sensation as if a vast number of stings were driven perpendicularly into them, [1] . ► Pain in the right index finger, as if it were forcibly pulled, [1] . ► Pricking, mingled with intolerable itching, in the fingers, [2] . keynotes.text: A. dracun. is commonly cultivated in gardens. It has a fetid smell which is apt to cause headache. The following effects of pounding the root and tearing up leaves and stems have been observed: Styptic metallic taste at back of mouth. Pricking, mingled with intolerable itching in fingers, as if hand plunged among nettles. On rubbing the fingers together, sensation as if a vast number of stings were driven perpendicularly into them. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
